title: Optional calculated duration-in-days when start and end dates are captured
description: When a form captures both a start date and an end date (e.g. *STDAT + *ENDAT, hospitalization start + discharge), optionally emit a calculate row with calculation `(decimal-date-time(${ENDDAT}) - decimal-date-time(${STDAT})) div 86400` (seconds-to-days conversion). Pair with `relevant: ${ONGO}='N'` so the duration only computes when there IS an end date. OPTIONAL — apply when the data consumer (PK analysis, exposure summary, AE-duration reporting) needs the derived value.
rationale: Many downstream analyses use duration-in-days (AE duration distributions, hospitalization-length comparisons, treatment-cycle exposure). Pre-computing it in the form makes the value available without a post-extract derivation step. Like 7J, this is opt-in per protocol/DMP rather than universal.

applies_when.soft: Form captures both a start date and an end date (STDAT/ENDAT pair or domain-specific equivalents like ADMITDAT/DISCHDAT), AND the data consumer requires duration as a derived value.

effect.soft: Emit a calculate row after the date pair: type=calculate, name=<base>DUR or DURATION_DAYS, label='Duration (days)', calculation=`(decimal-date-time(${<base>ENDAT}) - decimal-date-time(${<base>STDAT})) div 86400`, relevant=`${<base>ONGO}='N'` (skip when ongoing). Per OC-5b, if the duration is displayed to data entry, convert type=calculate to type=text + readonly=yes.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7K during Phase B.1c-final. Same fully-soft opt-in shape as 7J — applies_when and effect are both soft because the trigger condition requires protocol context. Note: the plan doc originally had 7K listed as 'CF helpers' based on partial reading. The actual prompts.py rule 7K is the duration-in-days calculation; CF helpers are rule 7L. Plan doc numbering corrected in the new OC7_decomposition_plan.md.
